rulings.Case Title: KING VANGA VS. JOHN DOES 1 TO 10
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Mandatory Attorney Fees In The Amount Of $67,405 And Costs In The Amount Of $2,504.59 Or As The Court Deems Just, Pursuant To Ccp 425.16; Memorndum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 10, 2026, Line 1, DEFENDANT KATHLEEN JUAREZ's Motion For An Award Of Mandatory Attorney Fees In The Amount Of $67,405 And Costs In The Amount Of $2,504.59 Or As The Court Deems Just, Pursuant To CCP 425.16. 1 - Continued on the court's own motion to February 26, 2026 to be heard with the fees motion of plaintiff Anthony Hidalgo. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: ANA MARIA CUEVAS VS. INTERNATIONAL TRAINING & EXCHANGE, INC. D/B/A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Entire Paga Action Without Prejudice; Declaration Of Joseph C. Ramli; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 10, 2026, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ANA CUEVAS, THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, AND OTHER AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES' Motion To Dismiss Entire PAGA Action Without Prejudice. The unopposed motion to dismiss is denied without prejudice. An individual settlement that dismisses PAGA claims without prejudice is a PAGA settlement that must be approved by the court. The moving papers provide no information from which the court can determine whether the abandonment of representative claims is fair to aggrieved absent employees, who may have foregone filing claims in light of the filing of this case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN TUCKER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 10, 2026, Line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The unopposed motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the causes of action for failure to provide a reasonable accommodation and failure to engage in the interactive process. Defendant provides evidence that it is entitled to judgment in its favor on both causes of action, shifting the burden to plaintiff to come forward with admissible evidence creating a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff has not opposed the motion and has not presented evidence creating a triable issue. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: KJELL SPANGBERG ET AL VS. GARY BAUER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 10, 2026, Line 9, PLAINTIFF KJELL SPANGBERG, KJELL SPANGBERG's APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. (Complete tentative ruling emailed to the parties). Hearing required. The court's tentative appears below. Moving party shall provide a proposed order on Judicial Council form AT-120 prior to the hearing if he has not already done so. Plaintiff's motion for a right to attach order and order for issuance of writ of attachment against defendant Gary Bauer is granted but the court stays its order for 10 days so that Bauer may provide an undertaking of $2 million. In the event Bauer has posted an undertaking by February 20, 2026, this order will be permanently stayed. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).
